items.text: Okay, the Birch Street Café just dropped a maple-cardamom latte and it's incredible! Only $4.50 and they donate fifty cents per cup to the campus food bank this week. Grab one before your 9 AM lecture in Hartman Hall. You will not regret it!

questions.stem: According to the post, how much does the new latte cost?
questions.explanation: The post states the maple-cardamom latte costs $4.50.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the café's donation offer?

options.A: It applies to every drink on the menu
options.B: It will continue throughout the spring term
options.C: It is a limited-time promotion ending soon
options.D: It replaces the café's regular pricing

questions.explanation: The post says the donation happens 'this week,' implying the offer is temporary and will end shortly.

items.text:
Hi Marcus,

Just a quick note that my office hours are shifting this week only. Instead of Tuesday afternoon, please come Thursday from 2:00 to 4:00 PM in Caldwell Hall, Room 318. I'm happy to review your thesis draft then.

Best,
Dr. Reyes

questions.stem: According to the email, when should Marcus visit this week?

options.A: On Thursday between two and four
options.B: On Tuesday between one and three
options.C: On Wednesday between three and five
options.D: On Friday between nine and eleven

questions.explanation: The email moves office hours to Thursday from 2:00 to 4:00 PM for this week only.

questions.stem: In the email, the word "shifting" is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: Here "shifting" means moving the office hours to a different time, so it is closest to 'changing.'

items.text: USED TEXTBOOK SALE! Saturday, May 10, in Lowell Hall, Room 112, from 10 AM to 3 PM. Hundreds of titles priced $5 to $25. Cash only. Sellers drop off books Friday afternoon. Get your fall semester reading before it's gone!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what is the price range of the books?

options.A: A range of two to ten dollars
options.B: A range of ten to fifty dollars
options.C: A range of fifteen to forty dollars
options.D: A range of five to twenty-five dollars

questions.explanation: The flyer states titles are priced from $5 to $25.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about buyers attending the sale?

options.A: They must register with the bookstore in advance
options.B: They can return unwanted books the next day
options.C: They are mostly graduating seniors selling old books
options.D: They should bring cash rather than a card

questions.explanation: Because the flyer says 'Cash only,' buyers can infer they need to bring cash instead of a card.

items.text: Free sunrise yoga in the Eastman Quad this Saturday at 8 AM! Bring your own mat, though the rec center lends spares for a $2 deposit. So relaxing before midterms! Instructor Priya keeps it beginner-friendly, so don't be shy. See you on the grass!

questions.stem: According to the post, where will the yoga class take place?

options.A: On the lawn of the Eastman Quad
options.C: On the roof of Eastman Hall
options.D: At the field beside the dormitories

questions.explanation: The post says the class is held in the Eastman Quad, on the grass.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who lack a mat?

options.A: They are not permitted to join the class
options.B: They must purchase a mat at the rec center
options.C: They can borrow one by leaving a small deposit
options.D: They should arrive an hour earlier than others

questions.explanation: The post notes the rec center lends spare mats for a $2 deposit, so mat-less students can borrow one.
